decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this MVP as positive evidence for token-type labels; only start a new project if the controller wants neural pruning/quantization validation beyond this retrieval-system test.
decision.stop_reason: Real retrieval-system evaluation exposed token-type failure clusters, demonstrated a targeted label-aware uplift above the success threshold, and the 75-label audit found no sampled weak-label artifacts.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this MVP as positive evidence for token-type labels; only start a new project if the controller wants neural pruning/quantization validation beyond this retrieval-system test.
run_notes_tail: - `src/real_retrieval_evaluation.py`: real lexical, query-typed, structural, and label-aware retrieval/extraction evaluator.
- Lexical window retriever aggregate accuracy: 7.53%; worst token type: 0.46%.
- Query-typed retriever aggregate accuracy: 33.60%; name=14.02%, number=24.14%, unit=100.00%, delimiter=39.20%.
- Structural extractor aggregate accuracy: 100.00%; all token-type slices at 100.00%.
- Label-aware extractor aggregate accuracy: 100.00%; all token-type slices at 100.00%.
- Targeted label-aware uplift over the query-typed retriever: +66.4 aggregate points and +86.0 worst-token-type points.
- Failure clusters below 60% accuracy: 7; token-type labels exposed especially severe name/number failures in the query-typed retriever and broad failures in naive lexical retrieval.
- Checked 75 stratified weak labels; pass rate 100.00%; issue counts: {}.
- This was an automated consistency/artifact audit rather than a human review, but it found no synthetic-template artifacts in the sampled labels after accounting for derived abstention answers.
- CPU retrieval run only; no GPU needed.
- Wall clock 0.280s; 22099 system predictions/sec; p95 latency per system is reported in `system_summary.csv`.

The real retrieval run upgrades the evidence from synthetic pass-rate simulation to executable system behavior. The labels still expose concrete failure clusters and point to a simple targeted fix: route exact-token questions to structural/label-aware extraction instead of similarity-style token retrieval. Because the dataset remains template-synthetic and the strongest extractor exploits those templates, this is positive MVP evidence rather than a claim about open-model pruning/quantization behavior.
